title: Extend /api/restore to invalidate CacheStore (master.db swap)
description: Restoring a Rekordbox backup replaces master.db. All cached analysis is stale (different content rows + possibly different ANLZ paths). /api/restore currently clears in-memory analysis caches and similar._INDEX; extend it to also call CacheStore.invalidate_all() and reopen against the new master.db.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/restore {filename} clears CacheStore atomically before returning success.
After restore, the next request triggers a full rebuild: tracks_snapshot, energy_curve, classification, etc.
tests/test_serve_routes.py restore case asserts the cache file is empty (via dump_schema + COUNT(*) checks) post-restore.
Backup file path validation (within BACKUP_DIR) is unchanged - no new security surface.
The /api/restore handler still: disposes db._engine, copies the backup file, reopens via Rekordbox6Database, updates app.state.db (existing behavior).

steps.details: serve/routes.py. Find the existing handler that disposes db._engine, copies file, reopens. After reopen, find the existing call to similar.clear_index() + analysis-cache clears.

After the existing in-memory clears, app.state.cache_store.invalidate_all(). The cache file path is keyed to <rekordbox_dir> which is unchanged by restore (master.db is replaced in-place) - so no reopen of CacheStore is needed; just invalidate. If restore relocates the rekordbox dir (it shouldn't), reopen CacheStore via CacheStore.open_for(new_dir).

tests/test_serve_routes.py: extend the restore test. Pre-populate CacheStore with mock energy_curve rows. POST /api/restore. Assert COUNT(*) FROM energy_curve == 0 after restore. Assert that get_energy_curve immediately after returns None (recomputes on next call).

technicalNotes: Per CLAUDE.md, /api/restore already calls similar.clear_index() to prevent stale feature vectors matching the new DB. This task extends that contract to the sidecar.
WAL/SHM sidecar files for master.db are handled by the existing restore path - cache is independent.
